records.m_pText: A Glamorous Life
This thing is enormous!$nHow many Eschalots do you$nthink would fit in there!?
Is it a ship?$nAn island?
Have we chanced upon the$nforbidden sanctuary of$nthe bikini tribe!?
Here’s hoping they’re cannibals$nwith a taste for philanderer.
From its look, it has to be a ship.$nSome luxury cruise liner, maybe?
A luxury what? Crew who?$nWhat are you talking about,$n[PCF2]?
It’s like a super-duper huge boat$nwith every extravagance imaginable!
Every cabin a king’s bedchamber,$nevery meal a five-star feast,$nyou name it, they have it aboard!
Whoa, such a thing really exists!?
And here I’ve never seen anything$nbigger than a crabbing ship!
[PCM1], please. You’re getting$nbumpkin all over my feral$nbikini woman fantasy.
The Shoreline Baths
Hey, Datz, what’s that smoke$nrising over the coast?
Oh, that’s steam from a natural$nhot spring. A vein of magma$nempties into the sea there,$nheating the water.
Wow, a real hot spring.
They say the waters work$nwonders on cuts and scrapes.$nEven burns.
And, uh… See that cape out there?$nThat one’s a mixed bath.$nEverybody in one big tub.
Ngh!?
Huh? [PCM2], where are you—
Wow, he’s nearly out of sight.$nHeck of a pair of legs on that guy.
Is it alright to let him go?$nWe’ll be hearing screams from$nthe cape in no time.
Naw, it’s fine.$nNothing to worry about.
Around this time…ah, that’s right.$nThe mix oughtta be mostly valtoras$nin the bath now. Bah ha ha!
Hey, now he’s sprinting back this way.$nHe’s shouting something…
Sounds like, “I’ll…get…you…Datz.”$nAh ha ha, and he’s crying!
Ah ha…ha…
Eleanor’s Home Cooking
So, what did you think of$nEleanor’s cooking?$nPretty great, right?
Absolutely! The sausage was$ntender and juicy.
The potatoes au gratin were like$na gooey dream!
It was all delicious.
Heh, sounds like she rolled out$nall the classics. They’re our$nwartime staples.
You eat them a lot?
As you can see, this isn’t the$nmost fertile patch of land.$nPotatoes are all that’ll grow.
And meat shortages mean we’re$nalways padding out the sausage$nwith something.
All the more impressive, then,$nthat such a feast came from$nmeager supplies.
No doubt it was her love for me$nthat gave it such flavor!
Yeah, I’ll bet…
I’m sure poor Eleanor’s got$nplenty of love stocked up by$nnow, with her husband away.
But the commander’s stuck out$non the front lines. He barely$ngets home, these days.
I just feel awful for her, stuck$nin that big house all alone…
Well, I hope we can pitch in to$nhelp her out somehow.
The Swordmaster’s Sting
That Kamiizumi…$nWe lost an entire squad to$nthe swordmaster alone.
Thinking back on it, we probably$nonly survived because he let us.
He seems all calm and peaceful,$nbut as soon as he draws that$nsword, he’s a hurricane.
Just talking about it has all the$nwounds he gave me throbbing…
And that same swordmaster and$ncommander of the mercenary$nBlack Blades is our$n[PCF2]’s teacher.
I’m sorry. I guess that’s another$nsecret I kept from you… But I$ndidn’t want to believe the$nstories.
If it is too painful for you, you$nare free to walk away, you know.
No one is forcing you to join us$nin this journey.
What?
[PCF1], you don’t have to$nsay it like that.
I fear I haven’t the time to$nhesitate or the leisure to$ndoubt. We must press forward.
Symbol of the Shieldbearers
Commander Goodman really lives up$nto his name, huh? Down to earth$nand understanding.
Right? Everybody here loves$nhim, soldiers and townsfolk$nalike. Respect him, too.
He’s the reason the Shieldbearers$nhave held together this long.$nHe’s the glue.
And he practices what he preaches.$nHe’s a paragon of self-defense.
We never fight to take anything$nfrom anybody else. Ever.
So, [PCM1]. Mind if I ask$nwhat’s got the vestal and$n[PCF2] all in a bunch?
Er, yeah… It’s kind of—
Girls are complicated things,$nand it’s a foolish man who$nasks too many questions.
Y-yeah? That certainly sounds$ncomplicated…
Tragedy
This town is awash with sadness.$nI never truly knew the depths$nof war’s tragedy…
It creates nothing,$nbrings happiness to no man…$nWhy do we persist in this madness?
Because both sides have beliefs$nthey can’t let go, I suppose.
Something so precious that it$nwarrants the deaths of$ncountless innocent people?
The world is faced with a crisis$ndire as it has ever seen, and$nthis is what we are doing?
Humans fight to secure peace as$nthey envision it. The trouble is,$neveryone’s vision is different.
I fear I cannot understand it…
However lofty the ideals of$nsword and shield, the fact$nremains that they are engaged$nin murder.
Wherever people gather,$nemotions run high, and justice$nand truth are subjective.
Where the majority has reached$nconsensus, it is difficult for$nany one person to resist.
Even so, I don’t want to be the$nsort of person who just accepts$nall that as a given.
Me neither.
At the very least, I swear I will$ntake no woman for granted.$nI’ll give my all, always.
Aaand there’s the sad, strange$n[PCM2] we know and love$nagain.
Keeping Up with [PCM2]
[PCF2]! Care to guess who I$nwas just with? Hmm? Curious!?
It’s quite the surpriiise!
Fine, fine, tell me about this$nwoman you met.
I’ll put up with you for a while.
So! Last night I chanced across$na most unusual lady…
She was crawling along the$nwalls and ceilings!
What!? What kind of girl was$nthis!?
I couldn’t say. An acrobat or$nsome such, I expect?
An acrobat…? Hmm, I suppose she$ncould have been practicing.
Ah, or perhaps she was on patrol!$nNoble girl, keeping her home safe.
You make her sound like a guard$ndog…gecko…thing. And wait,$nwhy were you in her house!?
Ah! Owww…
Just remembering the feel of her$nelbow strike has me all aflutter!
Oof. I can’t keep up…
Datz’s Name
Datz Strongberry is kind of an$nadorable name. Strawberry…$nHee hee hee!
Something funny about my name?$nWhat, I amuse you, girly? Huh?
Oh, er— N-no, I just… It’s a lovely$nname. Very unusual! Ha ha ha…$nI’m sorry. I meant no offense.
Bah ha ha! Just kidding!$nDid I lay it on a little thick?
I haven’t got mad at people$nmaking fun of my name since I$nsigned up. Eh, Zatz?
Not since you flattened two$nsquads worth of jokers and$ngot tossed in the brig.
Yeesh…
Ha ha, all four of us wound up$nthere on the day we enlisted.
Guess time mellows out even$nthe rowdiest of us, huh?
All four of you?
Yeah, it’s been the four of us$ntogether, for better or worse,$never since that first brawl.
Been awhile since I’ve seen$nSteiner and Neuer… You figure$nthey’re alright?
Sure, they’re with the$ncommander. Why don’t we show$nthe vestal around and drop by?
Airy vs. Egil
Egil, I’m—$nHey! Egil, I’m talking to you!$nHello!?
…Yeah, yeah. I hear you.$nQuit nagging me already.
Nag—!? And this, after you lied$nto me, you pip-squeak!
I’m telling you, I never lied.$nYou didn’t ask, so I didn’t$nmention it. That’s all!
Ohh, I don’t need to take that$nkind of back talk from you!
And I’m a lot bigger than you.$nYou’re the real pip-squeak.
What was that?
Nooothiiing! I didn’t say a word.
Honestly!$nWhat kind of attitude is that?$nAnd to a cryst-fairy, no less!
[PCF1], are you hearing this!?$nEgil is being horrid! [PCF1],$nplease!
……$nAiry, he’s a child.$nPlease do not be so immature.
Right? Right!?$nHa ha, you heard her, Airy.$nPfft heh heh heh…
Oh, you little—!
[PCF2]! [PCM2]!$n[PCM1]!!$nSomebody say something!!
Uhh… Yeah.
Hang…in there? I guess?
Wary Airy
Talk about a shock when Egil said$nhe wanted to come with us…
Although it’s not like there$nweren’t signs, too.
You could see it in his face as far$nback as when Commander Goodman$nsaid they’d take in the boys.
Hmm, I suppose, now that you$nmention it…$nHm? Airy…?
Is something wrong?$nYou’ve been staring off behind us.$nAre you expecting something?
……$nThe kid. Egil.
Egil? What of him?
There’s no way he stayed put!$nI won’t believe it,$nand I WON’T be tricked!
Ah ha ha! Once bitten,$ntwice shy, eh, Airy?
Hmm, he did seem to take those$norders from Eleanor awfully well,$nconsidering his usual tone.
Exactly. Something is fishy here,$nand I refuse to be duped!
You seem like you almost want$nhim to show up.
Ha ha ha, she and Egil have an$nepic rivalry going by now.
(You can’t fool me twice, brat…)
[PCM1]’s Thoughts
Something on your mind,$n[PCM1]?$nYou look pensive.
Hm? Oh. Yeah, a bit, I guess.
Thinking about Egil?
He’d just gotten settled at the$nGoodmans’, and now he’s being$nshipped off to a foreign land…
Yeah… There’s that, too.
That too…?
You mean Eleanor… Don’t you?
Yes. The two of them had$ndecided to take him in, and$nnow…
They had just enough time together$nto make it painful to part.$nI feel terrible.
I feel the same. She mustered a$nsmile to see him off,$nbut inside…
Can’t be helped, I’m afraid.$nWe can encourage him to write her$nonce he’s settled in Caldisla.
I wouldn’t mind ferrying the boy$nto the Goodmans’ every once in a$nwhile, either.
Ooh, seconded!
And aren’t we forgetting$nsomeone here?
We brought the boy along on a$nlot of good intentions and not$nmuch planning.
What happens if the innkeep says no?$nIt all hinges on him.
I might have a backup plan.$nLet’s pay the king a visit before$nthe two of them meet.
Zatz’s Name
What about my name?$nAnd why do you ask?
I know Zatz Mightee is an awfully$nimpressive moniker for$na little guy like me.
Er, no, that’s… That isn’t—
It’s fine, buddy. I don’t mind.$nDon’t worry about it.
Datz and I both. Our names are$nweirdly at odds with our builds.$nAlways have been…
We used to get teased a lot as$nkids, and we’d pound the kids$ndoing it. Normal kid stuff.
Ah ha ha, I can just picture it.
Having a name at all, knowing$nyour roots… I’d say that’s a$nblessing in itself.
Ahh, right. You lost all your$nmemories, didn’t you, [PCM2]?
Heh, I don’t even know if$n[PCM2] is supposed to be a$ngiven name or a family name.
Yes, very tragic.$nNow stop talking.
Datz and I always talked about$nhow we wanted to just ditch$nour names.
Wanted…in past tense?
Yeah… At my old man’s funeral,$neveryone who came by to talk to$nme said the same thing.
“He was a little man with the$nbiggest heart I’ve seen. He never$nran, even from a losing fight.”
“He was the sort of guy to walk$nright up to a bigger man and$nsock him one in the gut.”
My old man wasn’t big, but he$nwas sure as heck Mightee.
I could feel it, that day.$nSomething important, handed down$nfrom my grandpa, to him, to me…
It was like I could hear him$ntelling me it was my turn, now.$nMy time.
I understand that feeling well.$nAll vestals adopt the name of$nOblige.
Like you, we continue the chain,$nconnecting past and future.
Ah ha ha, golly.$nMy name’s nothing to compare$nto a vestal’s, but thanks.
Way I see it, something I didn’t$ncare about at all turned into$nsomething I like, that’s all.
And you still punch anybody who$nmakes fun of it just as hard.$nBah ha ha ha!
Hah! Ain’t that the truth.
Faltering Foreknowledge
Hmm…mmmrrrgh!
I can smell the smoke rising$nfrom that little brain of yours,$n[PCM2].
I’m at a loss. The writings in my$njournal are completely off the$nmark from what’s happening!
How can that be?
Oh, now that you mention it…
What did it say again? “The fat$none and the weakling make the$nbrat squeal by torture”?
What? Torture!? But—
Don’t get ahead of yourself,$n[PCM1].
My whole point is that the$ncontents of the journal have$nmissed the mark.
So what you thought was ironclad$nprophecy all this time maybe$nisn’t so ironclad.
Sounds like a crisis of faith to me.$nOr a crisis of superstition,$nI suppose.
We should be so lucky…
But I fear the journal’s failure$nto predict the future does not$nguarantee the child’s safety.
Hey, that’s right! He could be$nfacing even worse than torture!$nWe have to know for sure!
…Let us hurry.
R-right!
Rescuing Egil
Whew, am I glad to know$nhe’s safe.
Indeed. And he owes his safety$nto your bold action, [PCM1].
Bold and foolish, I should say.
I cannot deny that it was right$nto save the boy’s life.
But you have a higher calling,$n[PCM1]. The crystals’$nawakening hinges in part$nupon you.
Speaking of which, just what do$nyou suppose his connection to$nthe fire vestal was?
Zzz…zzz…
He’s out cold… Not that I can$nblame him, after all that.
Hey! Wake up, kid! You’re our one$nclue here. HEY!
Airy! Stop that.
At least let him rest until we$narrive in Hartschild.
Egil’s Words
Huh, whoa!
Hm? Are you alright!?
Yeah, sorry. Just stumbled a bit.
My eyes haven’t really adjusted.
All the more reason to be$ncareful where you step.
You know, when you think about it,$nthe moon’s way better than the sun.
What makes you say that?
The moon shines when it’s dark out,$nso we can see at night.
The sun only shines during the$nday when it’s bright anyway.
Ah ha ha, it’s bright during the$nday because of the sun, silly.
And did you know that the moon only$nshines because of the sun’s light?
Whoa, really? Huh…$nThe moon only shines because$nof the sun…
I guess that makes sense.$nIt must really be grateful!
Grateful? How so?
Because whenever the sun is tired,$nthe moon fills in for it while it rests!
A Place Where Time Lies Still
So this is where the fire vestal…
It’s just like my dream.$nAll of it…
It feels like time’s stopped here$nin the temple.
Yeah?
Perhaps because time has stopped$nfor the spirit of the fire vestal.
[PCF1]…
It’s kind of hard to breathe.
Is time stopping for me, too?$nWill we be alright?
We’re fine. Time moves on…
Our time is still moving, Egil.
This seems hard on the boy.
Then let us awaken the crystal$nand be quit of this place.
Ain’t That the Truth?
The proprietress put on a brave face,$nbut we make now for the duchy.$nEnemy territory.
Right.$nIt’s going to be dangerous.
Datz, Zatz, it’s best if you two$ntook the proprietress and left$nthe ship.
Though it kills me to say$nfarewell to her cooking.
No more desserts…
It can’t be helped, you two.$nBesides, these two have their$nmission as Shieldbearers.
Huh? Mission? Oh, right!$nHey, don’t worry about that.
Our current orders are to escort$nthe vestal of wind and keep her safe.
What? Since when?
Since you guys helped us out.$nThe orders came from$nCommander Goodman himself.
That’s why the two of us both came$nto Grandship to tip a few back.
The old house is full of the$nnew Shieldbearers who came to$ntake over for us.
Then I guess that just leaves$nthe proprietress. We’ll have to$nconvince her to take shelter.
Yeah, good luck with that.$nMaybe when Mount Karka$nfreezes over.
You’ll be lucky if she doesn’t$nbeat you for asking.
“I’m Grandship born and raised!$nIf you don’t like it, get the heck$noff my boat!”
Bah ha ha! Too perfect!$nThat’s her answer, guaranteed.
An uncanny likeness.
Still, the danger is too great.$nIt is for her own sake that we ask—
Look, my bet is that seeing you$nall stirred something inside her.$nInspired her.
Besides, you really think you$ncan live without her cooking$nnow that you’ve had it?
A fate grimmer than a thousand$ndeaths!
“Ain’t that the truth?”$n…Tee hee.
Bah ha ha! You’re not bad, yourself!$nLook, you leave her to us.
Between the three of us, we’ll$nkeep this boat safe when you’re$naway, no matter what!
…Then you have my thanks,$nDatz, Zatz.
Think nothing of it!$nYou just rest easy and leave this$ncrate to us!
The Ice of Eternia
Whoa, this is amazing!$nLook how high up we are!
I could gaze upon every woman$nin the world at once from$nGrandship’s deck!
I’m coming, my sweets!$nThere’s not a one of you more than$nten minutes away from me now!
The long wait is over!
Yours is a one-track mind, and$nthat track isn’t even a good one.
Still, it’s weird coming home$nafter so long.
Eternia, Land of Immortality…$nIt’s cold there year-round,$nright?
Right! And I’m not kidding when I$nsay cold. Bundle up, everybody!
[PCF2], what, um…$nWhat is snow like?
I fear I’ve never seen it before.
Well, for one, it’s at the top of$nmy “white as snow” list!
It’s like white fluffy crystals$nraining from the sky.
I see. That sounds quite$nromantic, actually.
It’s like bits of cotton candy$nfalling from the heavens.
It still melts on your tongue,$nbut it’s salty, not sweet.
Is…that so?
It does start as evaporated sea$nwater, after all.
Ah, of course! And the sea is$ncertainly salty.
And that’s not all. Sometimes,$nwhen it snows, fish fall from$nthe sky!
Fish? Really?
Ah ha ha, don’t tease the poor$ngirl, [PCM2]! Be nice.
You were deceiving me!?
Ha ha, sorry I didn’t pipe up sooner.$nIf you still have questions,$nI’ll give you real answers.
Honestly, [PCF2]!$nSo, what color is snow, really?
